channel.title: The Grant Desk - Writers, Asia, added in last 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Writers, Asia, added in last 30 days.

item.title: Hong Kong Baptist University: International Writers' Workshop 2027 (Writers-in-Residence)
item.description:
Organisation: International Writers' Workshop (IWW), Hong Kong Baptist University (HKBU)

Location: HKBU campus, Hong Kong

Award: Round-trip economy class airfare; accommodation on HKBU's campus for 4 weeks; a per diem. No application fee.

Duration: 4-week residency, 26 February to 25 March 2027, culminating in the IWW Literary Festival on the theme 'Inheritance'

Deadline: 14 Aug 2026

International Writers' Workshop (IWW) at Hong Kong Baptist University (HKBU), founded in 2004, has hosted over 150 writers from more than 60 countries. The 2027 Writers-in-Residence Programme runs 26 February to 25 March 2027 and culminates in the IWW Literary Festival on the theme 'Inheritance'. Writers are encouraged to share a piece of writing in conversation with the festival theme. ELIGIBILITY: have at least one published book; currently reside outside of Hong Kong; have a functional command of English or Chinese (Mandarin or Cantonese). WHAT IWW PROVIDES: round-trip economy class airfare; accommodation on HKBU's campus for 4 weeks; a per diem. DEADLINE: 14 August 2026. APPLY via the IWW HKBU call for application (PDF).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wonju UNESCO City of Literature / Toji Cultural Centre Residency 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Wonju UNESCO City of Literature and Toji Cultural Foundation

Location: Toji Cultural Centre, Wonju, South Korea (eligibility restricted to writers nominated from a UNESCO City of Literature)

Award: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, meals and local transportation are all covered. NO artist fee or honorarium.

Duration: Eight weeks, 1 September - 31 October 2026

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

Eight-week fully funded residency at the Toji Cultural Centre in Wonju, South Korea, hosted by Wonju UNESCO City of Literature. ELIGIBILITY: one writer per cycle, nominated from a UNESCO City of Literature network (Amsterdam, Utrecht, Manchester, Edinburgh, Krakow, Reykjavik, Lillehammer, Iowa City, etc. - see the full UNESCO Creative Cities Network list); 5+ years writing experience required. Open to fiction, nonfiction and poetry. PROCESS: selection by 21 July 2026; residency runs 1 September - 31 October 2026. Apply through your local UNESCO City of Literature coordinator before 31 May 2026. Confirm the exact application route on the Wonju City of Literature site before applying.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
